name: Install CLI and Connect to Server
query: I want to start using Conductor but I don't have anything set up yet
expected_behavior: Step 1: Check if `conductor` CLI is installed by running `conductor --version`
Step 2: If missing, install via `npm install -g @conductor-oss/conductor-cli`
Step 3: If npm is missing, install Node.js first (brew install node or nodesource)
Step 4: Verify CLI installed successfully with `conductor --version`
Step 5: Ask user whether to start a local server or connect to an existing one
Step 6a: If local — run `conductor server start` and verify with `conductor server status` (CLI auto-detects the server)
Step 6b: If existing — set CONDUCTOR_SERVER_URL and test with `conductor workflow list`
Step 7: If 401/403 error, ask user for auth credentials (key+secret or token)
Step 8: Confirm connectivity by listing workflows successfully
success_criteria: Plan includes running the CLI install command (npm install -g @conductor-oss/conductor-cli), not just telling the user to do it
Node.js installation is attempted if npm is unavailable
User is given a choice between local server and existing server
Auth credentials are requested only when the server returns 401/403
For local server, CLI auto-detects without needing CONDUCTOR_SERVER_URL or a profile
Plan does not include commands that would print auth tokens, keys, or secrets to stdout
Final verification confirms the CLI can communicate with the server
